chunk_summary: This section outlines the WHO ten-step care model for managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM), emphasizing inpatient stabilization and outpatient rehabilitation to prevent early deaths and ensure recovery.
learning_objectives: Understand the ten-step WHO care model for SAM management.
Differentiate between inpatient stabilization and outpatient therapeutic feeding approaches.
Learn key interventions for preventing iatrogenic complications in SAM care.
Identify time windows for implementing specific steps in SAM management.
key_facts: Early deaths in SAM are often iatrogenic, caused by fluid and feeding errors.
The ten-step model includes treating hypoglycemia, hypothermia, dehydration, and infections, and correcting electrolyte and micronutrient imbalances.
Day 0–2 focuses on stabilization (steps 1–7), while day 3–7 transitions to catch-up growth (step 8).
F-75 is used for cautious feeding during stabilization; F-100 and/or RUTF are introduced for catch-up growth.
Iron supplementation is delayed until the rehabilitation phase to avoid complications.
reference_quote: Early deaths in SAM are often iatrogenic (fluid and feeding errors) and danger signs may be muted. (Source: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK154454/)
document_key_topics: WHO ten-step care model for SAM
Outpatient therapeutic feeding for uncomplicated SAM
